element_choice.justification: Every element is the best semantic match: <details>/<summary> for expandable questions, <nav><ol> for breadcrumb, <search> for the filter, <label> for the input, <a href='tel:...'> for the phone number. No <dl> misuse for FAQ items and no inappropriate <address> usage.

aria_discipline.justification: ARIA used only where necessary: aria-label on <nav> and <search> landmarks (required to distinguish them), aria-labelledby on <section> elements, aria-current='page' on the breadcrumb current item, tabindex='-1' on <main> for skip-link targeting. No redundant ARIA anywhere.

heading_hierarchy.justification: Exactly one <h1> ('Frequently Asked Questions'), all three FAQ categories and 'Still need help?' correctly use <h2>, no levels skipped. Heading levels reflect document structure, not visual size.

landmark_structure.justification: Both skip links ('Skip to main content' and 'Skip to search') appear as the first focusable elements, appropriate for this page's complexity. <header>, <main>, <nav aria-label='Breadcrumb'>, <search>, and <section aria-labelledby> are all used correctly. <main> wraps only page-unique content; <header> is outside it.

form_semantics.justification: The search input has a visible <label> associated via 'for'/'id', uses type='search', and has a helpful placeholder that supplements (not replaces) the label. No issues for a single-field filter form.

list_semantics.justification: Breadcrumb correctly uses <ol> (order matters for position/hierarchy). No other list content on the page where counts would be relevant to users.

expectations_met: <main> wraps only the FAQ content — <header> is outside it
Both 'Skip to main content' and 'Skip to search' skip links are the first focusable elements
Individual questions use <details>/<summary> for progressive disclosure
Questions are NOT marked up as <dl>
The expandable pattern uses native <details>/<summary>, not custom ARIA disclosure
Contact link is an <a> element, phone number is an <a href='tel:+18005551234'>
No <address> misused for support contact info (correctly omitted)
additional_observations: Good use of the <search> landmark element for the filter, correctly labelled with aria-label to distinguish it as a landmark
aria-current='page' on the final breadcrumb item is a nice accessibility touch not explicitly required by the prompt
tabindex='-1' on <main> ensures the skip link target receives focus correctly in all browsers
